summary: Large warning-cleanup/header pass focused on eliminating implicit-function-declaration and related clang warnings across fighter, item, Dolphin/Runtime, MSL, MetroTRK, and baselib code. The PR added explicit prototypes/includes, introduced a clearer MUST_MATCH build define for matching-only paths, moved platform/compiler macros into a new Runtime/platform.h, converted several NON_MATCHING conditionals to MUST_MATCH-first structure, and removed one Ness assembly file by moving/matching code into C. Review discussion emphasized no-warning builds, wrapping platform-specific code instead of suppressing warnings, putting Doxygen briefs in headers, and making matching implementations appear first in #if/#else blocks.
what_changed: Added MUST_MATCH as a build-time define for matching builds: CFLAGS now adds -DMUST_MATCH when NON_MATCHING is not set, and ASFLAGS adds --defsym MUST_MATCH=1 for matching assembly paths.
Replaced several #ifndef NON_MATCHING / #ifdef NON_MATCHING conditionals with #ifdef MUST_MATCH / #else, including assembly data guards and matching-only inline assembly in Runtime and MSL code.
Introduced include/Runtime/platform.h to centralize platform/compiler macros such as MWERKS_GEKKO, ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN, AT_ADDRESS, and Gekko paired-single register identifiers qr0 through qr7.
Moved platform/compiler macro definitions out of include/dolphin/types.h and made dolphin/types.h include Runtime/platform.h instead.
Changed compile_flags.txt from suppressing unknown pragmas to treating them as errors by removing -Wno-unknown-pragmas and adding -Werror=unknown-pragmas.
Updated MetroTRK mem_TRK.h to use Runtime/platform.h and MWERKS_GEKKO rather than the previous M2CTX-style guard for __declspec(section ".init").
Reorganized src/MSL/trigf.c so MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementations for cosf, sinf, and __sinit_trigf_c appear first, with non-matching C implementations in #else branches.
Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_3.s, indicating at least ftNess_AttackHi4_Release_Action and associated data were no longer kept in that assembly file.

Added or moved prototypes/includes across many fighter and item files, including ftGameWatch, ftFox, ftLuigi, ftMewtwo, ftMasterHand, Ness, item headers, ft_unknown_006.h, and code_80081B38.h, to address implicit function declarations.
Adjusted documentation placement: reviewer comments note that Doxygen briefs were generally moved from implementation files into headers, while implementation details remained in C files.

Converted some raw zero/null usage and initializer forms for clarity/warnings, such as using NULL for callback clears and using grouped struct initializers when clang warned about sub-structure grouping.
Marked placeholder macros in dolphin/types.h with @todo Remove, including STRUCT_PLACEHOLDER, CALLBACK_PLACEHOLDER, and UNK_FUNC.
smart_moves: Introduced MUST_MATCH instead of relying on !NON_MATCHING, because the author explicitly noted that non-matching status and target/compiler identity are no longer equivalent once non-target-platform builds matter.
Used a platform header to isolate compiler/architecture-specific macros, reducing warning noise in shared headers and making it easier for clang/LSP tooling to reason about cross-platform code.
Preferred wrapping platform-specific code in correct #if guards over suppressing clang warnings globally; compile_flags.txt moved toward stricter diagnostics rather than broader exemptions.
Placed matching implementations first in #if/#else blocks, which review discussion said reads better and makes the canonical decomp target easier to see.
Preserved matching-only hacks behind MUST_MATCH, such as the Fox double assignment and Gecko_ExceptionPPC unused asm peephole trigger, while allowing cleaner code in non-matching builds.
Moved extern function declarations into headers where possible, reducing local ad-hoc externs and making dependencies searchable/reusable.
Documented suspected shared inline issues with @todo comments instead of hiding them, for example Fox blaster shot and Mewtwo Teleport action shared-inline notes.
Kept no-warning goals aligned with decomp work: warning fixes were not just cosmetic, because implicit function declarations can hide wrong signatures and produce misleading compiler behavior.
decomp_lessons: Do not use !NON_MATCHING as a synonym for matching-target code. Matching-build requirements should be expressed with a positive MUST_MATCH define, especially when the repo also supports host or analysis builds.
Implicit-function-declaration cleanup is primarily a header/prototype correctness task: add the right include or shared header instead of repeating local extern declarations.

When a matching-only construct is fake or compiler-bug-related, gate it explicitly and leave a short evidence-based comment. This keeps the non-matching path readable without losing the matching tactic.
For files with matching asm and non-matching C alternatives, put the matching implementation first under MUST_MATCH, then place portable/non-matching C under #else.
Centralize platform attributes and compiler extensions in a small platform header so general type headers do not accumulate target-specific pragmas and macros.

Treat clang warning cleanup as useful decomp feedback: warnings can reveal missing prototypes, wrong function signatures, ungrouped struct initializers, platform-specific pragmas, and local externs that should live in headers.
Use Doxygen headers for public usage information and keep implementation details in .c files; Doxygen can combine both, but header briefs are more reusable.
Commented-out matching code should not remain as loose comments; review discussion accepted moving such code behind #if FALSE when it documents a matching-but-not-DOL-ready state.
naming_conventions: MUST_MATCH: positive define for code paths that must match the original target output.
NON_MATCHING: retained for non-matching builds but discouraged by the author as an architecture/compiler proxy.
MWERKS_GEKKO: shorthand for defined(__MWERKS__) && defined(GEKKO), regardless of whether the target must match.
Runtime/platform.h: central location for platform/compiler macros such as ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN, AT_ADDRESS, and qr0-qr7.
Doxygen brief comments should live near declarations in headers; implementation notes and scratch/matching details should remain in source files.
Use NULL for pointer callback clearing rather than literal 0 where the value is a pointer, as seen in ftKirby callback cleanup.
Use @todo for unresolved placeholder or matching issues, including placeholder macros marked for future removal.
Use @remarks for warning-style notes such as the GigaKoopa uninitialized-offset UB warning.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Makefile now defines MUST_MATCH for assembly via ASFLAGS --defsym MUST_MATCH=1, enabling assembly sources to use .ifdef MUST_MATCH instead of .ifndef NON_MATCHING.
OSError_data.s changed from .ifndef NON_MATCHING to .ifdef MUST_MATCH, matching the new positive build-guard style.
MSL trigf.c keeps inline asm versions of cosf, sinf, and __sinit_trigf_c under MUST_MATCH while retaining C implementations for non-matching builds.
Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c keeps an unused static asm function under MUST_MATCH to trigger a known peephole-off compiler behavior.

ftFox_CreateBlasterShot keeps a matching-only suspicious double assignment, fp = fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj), behind MUST_MATCH, with a note that it is probably fake or caused by missing inline functions.
Ness yoyo work removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_3.s and used C-side data such as static Vec3 const YoyoChargePos = { 0 }, indicating assembly/data migration into C.
Several functions were annotated with @todo Shared inline, including Fox blaster and Mewtwo Teleport code, preserving likely inline-sharing hypotheses for future matching work.
The PR treated local extern removals as matching-support work: replacing implicit/local declarations with headers improves signature stability before further matching attempts.

review_feedback.feedback: Author explained that MWERKS_GEKKO is only a shorthand for __MWERKS__ && GEKKO, while MUST_MATCH and NON_MATCHING differ in whether the target must match. The author wanted to phase out NON_MATCHING as a semantic proxy.
Author preferred wrapping platform-specific #ifs rather than ignoring warnings, and compile_flags.txt moved from -Wno-unknown-pragmas to -Werror=unknown-pragmas.
For ftFox_SpecialN.c, author said a matching-only odd line existed because the original line generated a clang warning and that aiming for no warnings/errors was reasonable.
Author noted that short member/function briefs were generally moved to headers, leaving usage information in headers and implementation details in C files.
Author said #if/#else order was swapped in ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c because matching implementation first reads better.
In ftgamewatch.h, comment notes that some static declarations were removed, implying header declarations should reflect externally needed functions rather than stale local-static assumptions.
In ftkirby.c, author changed commented matching code into #if FALSE, preserving it without leaving raw commented-out code.
For ftMario_SpecialLw.c, author said clang warned about not grouping sub-structures and accepted grouped initializers as clang-tidy/LSP-friendly.
Author was uncertain about Doxygen module mechanics for OSReset and ftMasterHand dir.dox and deferred deeper module cleanup to a future PR.
For Mewtwo SpecialHi, author noted there are many shared inline cases and they are hard to detect automatically, so only some were marked.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that phase out NON_MATCHING or further standardize MUST_MATCH.
Search for remaining local extern function declarations in fighter and item source files.
Audit Runtime/platform.h users for platform-specific #if wrapping and unknown pragma compatibility.
Check whether Doxygen module handling for OSReset or ftMasterHand dir.dox was resolved in later PRs.
Search for remaining @todo Shared inline comments added around Fox blaster and Mewtwo Teleport code.
Verify whether the deleted ftNess_3.s functions and sdata constants fully matched after migration to C.
Review later changes to compile_flags.txt for continued no-warning policy expansion.
Search for remaining #if FALSE matching-code blocks and whether their data was later moved for DOL match.
